steps.title: Recognize that population change depends on density change
steps.content:
Since Population = Density times Area, and the region's land area is constant at 91,600 square kilometers, the population increase equals (change in density) times (constant area).

Change in density: $3.0 - 2.3 = 0.7$ people per square kilometer

steps.title: Multiply the change in density by the constant area
